title: Structured Logging Framework
description: No unified logging system — logger.debug/info/error scattered without unified schema or structured format
impact: Debugging and production maintenance difficulty — logs cannot be analyzed automatically
proposed_solution: Adopt structlog or python-json-logger with unified schema for each layer
evidence: main.py:13 basicConfig time-only format; every file uses logging.getLogger(__name__); 300+ logging calls across src/ without JSON formatting

title: OpenTelemetry Distributed Tracing
description: No tracing linking requests across L3→L8 — correlation_id exists in TransportContext but is not actually used for OTel span/trace
impact: Difficulty identifying bottlenecks and root cause of failures in multi-layer pipelines
proposed_solution: Add OpenTelemetry instrumentation middleware at L7 entry point (FabricClient.send/receive) and pass context through layers
evidence: transport/context/transport_context.py:30 carries correlation_id; base.py:55 generates corr-{time_ns()}; 47 grep matches for correlation_id across src/ but no OTel spans

title: Prometheus Metrics Endpoint
description: No metrics (message count, latency percentiles, error rate per protocol, active sessions)
impact: Cannot monitor system health in production or create alerts
proposed_solution: Add Prometheus client with counters/gauges/histograms for each layer
evidence: wiring/pipelines/base.py:45 _stats (requests, errors, avg_latency_ms) in memory only; transport/channel/types.py:30 ChannelMetrics exists but not exported; fabric_health.py returns simple status only

title: Health Check Probes (K8s)
description: liveness_check/readiness_check exist in CommunicationFabricAssembler but do not actually check underlying components — no circuit breaker, no channel pool health
impact: /ready may return OK while channel pool or session registry is broken
proposed_solution: Link liveness_check to event loop health and circuit breaker; link readiness_check to channel pool health_check (pool_adapter.py:68), session registry and each registered pipeline
evidence: wiring/assembler.py:287-295 liveness only checks {_started, loop_running}; readiness:298-313 only checks pipeline count; adapters/fabric_health.py:28 provides FastAPI endpoints but depends on ICommunicationGateway which lacks liveness/readiness in contract

title: Audit Logging
description: No logging of incoming/outgoing messages for audit and compliance purposes
impact: Cannot track what communicated with what and when in regulated environments
proposed_solution: Add optional AuditLogger enabled via config — logs source,destination,protocol,session_id,payload_size,timestamp
evidence: No audit calls or message logging in any pipeline; inbound.py/outbound.py do not log payload metadata

title: Logger Overhead in Hot Path
description: PresentationPipeline.encode/decode calls logger.debug on every invocation causing cumulative performance impact on high-throughput paths
impact: Cumulative performance impact on high-throughput paths
proposed_solution: Replace logger.debug with isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG) or remove hot-path logging
evidence: presentation/pipeline.py:104 logger.debug in encode; :127 logger.debug in decode — called for every message

title: Unified Error Response Schema
description: No unified error schema across protocols — each protocol handler returns errors in different formats
impact: Difficult error processing at API gateway and client SDK; cannot analyze failure patterns
proposed_solution: Create ErrorResponse Pydantic model with code, message, layer, correlation_id, timestamp; unify across all protocol handlers
evidence: protocols/error_mapper.py exists but not used consistently; PipelineExecutionError (base.py:22) has layer/message/original but not unified as HTTP/gRPC response; logger.error sometimes with exc_info=True sometimes without

title: Channel Metrics Not Exported
description: ChannelMetrics (messages_sent, bytes_sent, errors) exists in transport/channel/types.py but not aggregated or exported to any monitoring system
impact: Cannot monitor individual channel performance or detect memory leaks in pool
proposed_solution: Aggregate ChannelMetrics via ChannelPool and expose via Prometheus endpoint or health check
evidence: transport/channel/types.py:30-39 ChannelMetrics dataclass (opened_at, closed_at, messages_sent, bytes_sent, errors); channel.py:45-46 property; but pool_adapter.py does not collect metrics and no exporter exists

title: ICommunicationGateway Contract Missing Liveness/Readiness
description: FabricHealthEndpoints calls gateway.liveness_check and gateway.readiness_check but ICommunicationGateway protocol does not declare them — only works by coincidence via structural typing
impact: Runtime crash if FabricClient interface changes; unclear to new developers
proposed_solution: Add liveness_check() and readiness_check() to ICommunicationGateway interface with documentation
evidence: wiring/contracts/communication_gateway.py:45 only health_check declared; adapters/fabric_health.py:56,59 calls gateway.liveness_check and readiness_check as structural typing only

title: Structured Log Correlation
description: correlation_id passes through TransportContext in every request but is not injected into logs — cannot link log entries to specific requests
impact: Debugging requires manual log search; cannot trace request journey across layers
proposed_solution: Add logging.Filter or setLogRecordFactory to inject correlation_id/session_id from context vars into every log record
evidence: transport/context/transport_context.py:30 correlation_id in every context; but logging.getLogger(__name__) in every file does not carry context metadata

title: Session Registry Lacks Observability Metrics
description: SessionRegistry (session_registry.py) does not log counters for created sessions, ended sessions, evicted sessions, or expired sessions
impact: Cannot monitor session leaks or usage patterns; difficulty in capacity planning
proposed_solution: Add internal counters (created, evicted, expired, active) and export via health check or Prometheus
evidence: session_registry.py: register() only logs; evict_idle() keeps no counters; active_count() only; no total_created, total_evicted
